mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand contraction, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rapid growth in industrial production outpaces capacity constraints, triggering inflation fears and monetary tightening expectations that generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 5-day returns specifically during late-cycle periods, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP reflects robust economic expansion and rising corporate profits, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative or flat 60-day forward returns in more than half of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a falling unemployment rate creates a stagflationary pressure environment that forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and UNRATE shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with a coefficient p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related supply chains, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of instances following a HOUST decline greater than 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, rapid monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strong labor market absorption and rising wage income, which fuels consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, with a hit rate below 50 percent across rolling 3-year windows.
